home.subtitle: Information Systems Engineer
home.tagline: A creative problem-solver passionate about building reliable systems and exploring the intersection of the arts, technology, and philosophy.
home.quote: "There is no dark side of the moon, really. As a matter of fact, it's all dark."
home.quoteSource: No Dark Side Works - Inspired by a sentiment from Pink Floyd's The Dark Side of the Moon.

about.heading: The Engineer, The Educator, The Architect of What's Next
about.p1: For some, a career path is a straight line. For Jorge Garro, it has been a journey of pragmatic adaptation, driven by a deep sense of purpose. From corporate enterprise systems to the classroom and back to pure engineering, his path reveals a professional who is part engineer, part educator, and a true problem-solver at heart, now poised to build the next chapter of his career in software development.
about.p2: The journey began not with code, but with sound. An early dream to become a sound engineer led him to study electronics, drawn to the hardware and circuits behind the music. Life and professional demands required a practical pivot. Influenced by his father, a self-taught systems analyst, Jorge transitioned to Information Systems, launching a successful career in corporate IT where he supported complex systems for major enterprises.
about.p3: Seeking a new challenge that would leverage his expertise in a different way, Jorge embraced an opportunity to step into the world of education. As an instructor in digital media and software development, he discovered a passion for mentorship, translating complex technical topics into foundational lessons for the next generation. This experience honed his ability to communicate with clarity and purpose, skills that are invaluable in any technical team.
about.p4: Through all these transitions, a core philosophy has been his guide. "I'm always driven by helping others, to define problems and solving them," he explains. This ethos of service is complemented by a powerful technical principle he draws from his passion as a musician. "Everything has a structure," he says. "There are patterns that when used correctly, can create beautiful amazing things... but it's always important not to add what is not necessary." This belief in elegant, essential design is the hallmark of a true engineer.
about.p5: Today, Jorge is preparing for his next evolution. With a goal of becoming a full-stack developer or data engineer, he isn't focused on a single trendy technology stack. "I want to be somebody who understands the whole system, front to back, back to front," he asserts. His passion lies in the complex, foundational challenges of back-end development and data engineering.
about.p6: Jorge Garro's story is not just about changing jobs; it's about a deliberate and purposeful navigation of a multi-faceted career. It's a testament to a professional who learns by researching, creating, and teaching, all while engineering a better future.

contact.heading: Contact Me
contact.body: I'm always open to discussing new projects, creative ideas, or opportunities to be part of an ambitious vision.

architecture.heading: Site Architecture
architecture.intro: This website is a full-stack application built with the PERN stack (PostgreSQL, Express, React, Node.js) and deployed on Vercel's serverless platform. It follows the Model-View-Controller (MVC) design pattern, and includes a full admin panel with authentication and a custom resume variant system. Here is a breakdown of each layer:

frontend.title: 1. Front-End (View)
frontend.body: A React 18 single-page application styled with Tailwind CSS and hosted on Vercel's global Edge Network. It includes fully bilingual support (EN/ES) via react-i18next, with public-facing pages for Resume, Blog, Projects, and About, and a protected admin panel for managing all site content. The resume page supports live PDF and ATS plain-text downloads generated on the server.

backend.title: 2. Back-End (Controller & Serverless Functions)
backend.body: The back-end is built with Node.js and Express, deployed as Vercel Serverless Functions. It exposes a REST API for all public and admin operations. The resume endpoint uses pdfkit to generate ATS-optimized PDF files on demand. A resume variant system allows creating targeted resume configurations — custom summaries, reordered skill categories, and filtered experience entries — applied at render time without duplicating content.

database.title: 3. Database (Model)
database.body: A Neon PostgreSQL database, accessed via non-pooling connections optimized for serverless cold-start environments. The Sequelize ORM manages seven tables: Resumes, Experiences, Education, Skills, Posts, ProjectConfigs, and ResumeVariants. JSONB columns store structured data such as contact info and variant config alongside standard relational associations.

admin.title: 4. Admin Panel & Authentication
admin.body: A protected admin panel at /admin handles all content management. Authentication uses JWT tokens — a signed token is issued on login and verified server-side on every admin API request. The panel includes a Resume Editor (header, experience, education, skills with bilingual support), a Resume Variants manager for creating targeted job-application versions, a Posts Manager, and a Projects Manager.
